title: DORA Outsourcing Registers — Sub-Processor Notification Lag vs Contractual Notice Period

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.5 — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that DORA Art.5 (ICT risk-management governance) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.3.32: DORA Outsourcing Registers — Sub-Processor Notification Lag vs Contractual Notice Period.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.3.32' running on sourcetype vendor:subproc_notice and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk DB Connect, email ingestion (careful PII controls)
spl:
index=vendor sourcetype="vendor:subproc_notice" earliest=-400d
| eval notice=strptime(notice_received_at,"%Y-%m-%d"), eff=strptime(effective_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| join type=left provider_id, subprocessor_name [
    | inputlookup roi_subprocessor.csv
    | eval reg=strptime(registered_at,"%Y-%m-%d")
    | fields provider_id, subprocessor_name, reg
  ]
| eval lag_days=round((reg-notice)/86400,1)
| where lag_days>14 OR isnull(reg)
| table provider_id, subprocessor_name, notice_received_at, registered_at, lag_days
description: Outsourcing arrangements require visibility into sub-processing changes. Comparing provider sub-processor announcement dates to your internal register update timestamps shows contractual notification periods were honored and documented.
value: Outsourcing arrangements require visibility into sub-processing changes. Comparing provider sub-processor announcement dates to your internal register update timestamps shows contractual notification periods were honored and documented.

grandmaExplanation: Outsourcing arrangements require visibility into sub-processing changes — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk DB Connect, email ingestion (careful PII controls).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=vendor` `sourcetype="vendor:subproc_notice"` (provider_id, subprocessor_name, effective_date, notice_received_at); `roi_subprocessor.csv` (provider_id, subprocessor_name, registered_at).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Set `14` to your contractual days; (2) legal owns register updates—Splunk flags drift; (3) exclude immaterial vendors via lookup; (4) integrate CLM tool when available; (5) export for RoI reconciliation.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vendor sourcetype="vendor:subproc_notice" earliest=-400d
| eval notice=strptime(notice_received_at,"%Y-%m-%d"), eff=strptime(effective_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| join type=left provider_id, subprocessor_name [
    | inputlookup roi_subprocessor.csv
    | eval reg=strptime(registered_at,"%Y-%m-%d")
    | fields provider_id, subprocessor_name, reg
  ]
| eval lag_days=round((reg-notice)/86400,1)
| where lag_days>14 OR isnull(reg)
| table provider_id, subprocessor_name, notice_received_at, registered_at, lag_days
```

Understanding this SPL

**DORA Outsourcing Registers — Sub-Processor Notification Lag vs Contractual Notice Period** — Outsourcing arrangements require visibility into sub-processing changes. Comparing provider sub-processor announcement dates to your internal register update timestamps shows contractual notification periods were honored and documented.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=vendor` `sourcetype="vendor:subproc_notice"` (provider_id, subprocessor_name, effective_date, notice_received_at); `roi_subprocessor.csv` (provider_id, subprocessor_name, registered_at). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk DB Connect, email ingestion (careful PII controls). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vendor; **sourcetype**: vendor:subproc_notice. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vendor, sourcetype="vendor:subproc_notice", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **notice** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Joins to a subsearch with `join` — set `max=` to match cardinality and avoid silent truncation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **lag_days** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where lag_days>14 OR isnull(reg)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **DORA Outsourcing Registers — Sub-Processor Notification Lag vs Contractual Notice Period**): table provider_id, subprocessor_name, notice_received_at, registered_at, lag_days


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (lags), Single value (breach count), Timeline.
